item: The main claims made in the abstract and introduction accurately reflect the paper's contributions and scope.
All claims are supported by theoretical analysis or experimental evidence presented in the paper.
The paper discusses the limitations of the work performed.
The paper discusses potential negative societal impacts of the work performed.
All assumptions made in theoretical analysis are clearly stated.
Complete proofs of all theoretical results are included in the paper or supplementary materials.
The paper specifies all the training and test details necessary to understand the results.
The paper reports error bars or confidence intervals for all experimental results.
The paper specifies the number of runs or trials for each experiment.
The paper specifies all hyperparameters used, including learning rate, batch size, and optimizer settings.
The paper specifies the random seeds used for initialization and data shuffling.
The paper specifies the exact number of training epochs or iterations.
The paper specifies the early stopping criteria if used.
The paper specifies the learning rate schedule or decay strategy.
The paper specifies the regularization techniques used (dropout, weight decay, etc.).
The paper specifies the data augmentation techniques used if any.
The paper specifies the exact dataset name and version used.
The paper specifies the dataset split ratios for train, validation, and test sets.
The paper specifies how the data was preprocessed or normalized.
The paper specifies the total number of samples in each dataset split.
The paper provides statistics about the class distribution or label balance.
The paper provides a complete description of the model architecture.
The paper specifies the number of parameters in the model.
The paper specifies the activation functions used.
The paper specifies the loss function used for training.
The paper specifies the weight initialization method.
The paper specifies the type of compute resources used (GPU model, CPU specs).
The paper specifies the total training time or wall-clock time.
The paper specifies the memory requirements (GPU memory, RAM).
The paper specifies the deep learning framework and version used.
The paper specifies all evaluation metrics used and how they are computed.
The paper compares against relevant baseline methods.
The paper uses the same evaluation protocol as prior work for fair comparison.
The paper reports results on standard benchmark datasets.
The paper provides access to the source code.
The paper provides documentation or instructions for running the code.
The paper specifies the software dependencies and versions required.
The paper provides pre-trained model weights or checkpoints.
The paper provides access to the dataset or explains how to obtain it.
The paper describes how hyperparameters were selected or tuned.
The paper specifies the validation set used for model selection.
The paper reports whether the best model was selected based on validation performance.
The paper includes pseudocode or algorithm descriptions for key methods.
